channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Todd Blanche’s loyalty to Trump questioned in Senate confirmation hearing
item.description: Blanche's actions have raised questions over the independence of the Department of Justice, which he currently leads.

item.title: Why are right-wing parties gaining ground in Latin America?
item.description: Several recent elections have strengthened conservative forces across parts of Latin America.

item.title: Argentine VP calls England ‘pirates’ ahead of World Cup semifinal clash
item.description: Argentina vice president's remarks made ahead of the World Cup semifinal against England and amid sovereignty dispute.

item.title: Cruise ship hotel left listing in China port after high tides
item.description: A cruise ship-turned-luxury floating hotel that has been idle for about 14 years rolled onto its side while docked.

item.title: Zelenskyy backs state energy company chief Koretskyi for new Ukraine PM
item.description: Ukraine's parliament is expected to vote for the next prime minister on Thursday.

item.title: Norway’s Waerenskjold wins fastest Tour de France stage in history
item.description: At an average speed of 50.91kph, Soren Waerenskjold's sprint was the fastest road stage in the 113 editions of the Tour.

item.title: Starmer bids MPs goodbye as British PM and pledges to support his successor
item.description: Andy Burnham, who is set to formally replace Starmer on Monday, will become the UK’s seventh prime minister in a decade.

item.title: Father, mother, daughter killed in Israeli attack on Gaza home
item.description: Palestinians held a funeral for a father, a mother and their six-year-old daughter killed in an Israeli air attack.

item.title: World Cup: Spain and Barcelona star Yamal’s home hit by attempted burglary
item.description: Lamine Yamal's targeted home was formerly owned by Barca great Gerard Pique and Colombian music star Shakira.

item.title: Revamped Cricket World Cup format could see an additional India-Pakistan match
item.description: The 14-team 2027 ODI tournament in Africa will have a Super Seven stage but no quarterfinals.

item.title: Atlanta police gear up for trouble at England vs Argentina World Cup match
item.description: The host city will have advanced security in place as a proactive measure against any potential crowd trouble between fans.

item.title: Pro-government militia seizes boat with ‘Houthi weapons materials’ in Yemen
item.description: A pro-government militia said it intercepted a boat carrying ‘weapon-making equipment for the Houthis’ in Yemen.

item.title: Trump urges ICE to keep using traffic stops after deadly incidents
item.description: US president backs tactic despite agency announcing temporary pause after fatal shootings in Texas and Maine.

item.title: Who is calling the shots in Iran amid the deadly war with the US?
item.description: The IRGC has taken the lead in Iran, particularly over Hormuz - and the government says it's on the same page.

item.title: England vs Argentina LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinal
item.description: Live coverage and text updates as England and Argentina renew one of football's great rivalries in the semifinal.

item.title: July 15, 10 years on: Turkiye’s will, Turkiye’s victory
item.description: The defeat of the 2016 coup attempt helped usher in a decade of greater diplomatic, economic and military power.

item.title: How US-Iran escalation will test Iraq’s balancing act
item.description: The Iraqi PM visited the White House to deepen economic ties, as Iraq-based allies of Iran threatened to fight the US.

item.title: My Twitter, not X
item.description: As Twitter, then X, marks 20 years since its launch, a journalist journeys through revolutions and wars.

item.title: Legal challenge says Trump ICC sanctions tread on US citizens’ rights
item.description: Groups charge that sanctions against ICC, Palestinian rights organisations stifle freedom of speech and expression.

item.title: Iraq’s prime minister carries the title, but not the power
item.description: Al-Zaidi’s Washington visit exposed the pressure reshaping Baghdad’s politics, security and economy.

item.title: What DOGE saved, and what it cost
item.description: The US government project that slashed foreign aid died on July 4. AJLabs looks at the cost in lives around the world.

item.title: World Cup final Lamine Yamal: What to know about teenage Spain prodigy
item.description: One of the best players in the world, Spain's 19-year-old Lamine Yamal, is gearing up for his first World Cup final.

item.title: Far-right Israeli lawmaker raids Palestinian school and threatens shutdown
item.description: Israel’s education committee head stormed a school in Jerusalem, vowing to shut it down over Palestinian symbols.

item.title: Protest in Pakistan’s Kashmir stalls after nine killed in clashes
item.description: At least 28 people have been killed since protests began in June, as the region braces for a major protest march.

item.title: Spain’s Oscar-winning actor raises Palestine flag at World Cup match
item.description: Spain's Oscar-winning actor Javier Bardem had a message for Palestinians as he raised Palestine flag during World Cup.
